label: Synthetic Realness
author: A. Jacobs
canonical_definition: Synthetic Realness describes a condition where something feels real because it is polished, coherent, emotionally legible, or aesthetically convincing, rather than because it remains grounded in reality.
short_definition: When something feels real because it is convincing, not because it is grounded.
core_claim: The appearance of realness can become detached from reality itself.

mechanisms.name: signal_replication
mechanisms.effect: systems reproduce the cues people associate with authenticity

mechanisms.name: aesthetic_optimization
mechanisms.effect: surfaces become smoother and more convincing than the realities behind them

mechanisms.name: emotional_legibility
mechanisms.effect: content becomes easier to feel before it is verified

mechanisms.name: grounding_loss
mechanisms.effect: the relationship between presentation and lived reality weakens

observable_patterns: content feels authentic but oddly generic
emotional cues appear without lived context
AI media looks emotionally convincing without depicting real events
corporate language sounds human while avoiding accountability
credibility is inferred from coherence and presentation

connects_to.description: Synthetic Realness appears when Reality Drift makes convincing representation easier than grounded contact.
Synthetic Realness increases when meaning is preserved aesthetically but loses grounding.
Synthetic Realness increases the burden of distinguishing grounded reality from convincing surfaces.
Synthetic Realness often emerges when systems optimize authenticity cues instead of actual grounding.
Constraint Collapse allows synthetic realness to spread without correction from reality.

examples.example: AI-generated images look emotionally convincing while depicting no real event.
Brand messaging performs sincerity without meaningful commitment.
Influencer content is staged to appear candid.
Corporate language sounds human while avoiding human accountability.

signature_lines: Synthetic Realness is what happens when the signs of reality become easier to produce than reality itself.
Something can feel real without being grounded.
The artificial becomes smoother than the real.
Polish becomes a substitute for presence.
search_queries: why everything feels fake but looks real
why AI images feel real but fake
